mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during structural labor shortages.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which directly lifts short-term equity valuations through improved earnings momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product confirms sustained macroeconomic growth, increasing corporate profit margins and equity risk premium compression, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.015 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 300.0, elevated inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 300.0, or the threshold effect disappears when inflation expectations are anchored below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the interaction term yields negative alpha in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader credit tightening, which reduces construction sector earnings and drags down S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative returns when government infrastructure spending offsets private residential weakness, or the signal shows positive predictive power during housing supply shortages.
